ENGNew: Draft for the Guest Speaker's Address
changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "A Record of Opening Remarks"

TextENGNew: Chairman,@kDistinguished members,@kCongratulations on the twenty-fifth anniversary of our study group's founding.
changeReason: Old renders 勉強会 as 'XX Party' but it means 'study group'; new correctly translates.

TextENGNew: These twenty-five years are themselves a history of this prefecture's development.

What was once a quiet landscape of endless fields@khas been transformed by the long-awaited opening of a bullet train station@kand the development of the expressway into a modern city brimming with youthful vitality.
An economy and industry that flourish by continually embracing the best of the new.

And through the deep love you all hold for your homeland, cherishing its finest traditions,@kthis city has grown into one of Japan's foremost, achieving a harmony of tradition and culture, economy and industry.
Indeed, the history of this prefecture's development is, in every sense, the history of this group's development.
changeReason: Old introduces causation ('couldn't have happened without') not present in the Japanese; the original states the prefecture's development history IS the group's development history, which the new translation captures.

TextENGNew: We are called upon to pursue every policy and pledge we adopt with unwavering resolve,@kwith the sharp, arrow-like decisiveness the word implies.
The members of this study group, who bear the arrow as their emblem, are themselves such arrows -- piercing through every obstacle in pursuit of the people's lasting happiness.
changeReason: Old mistranslates 会章にあしらった (incorporated into the group emblem) as 'fundamental bases' and misses 恒久的 (lasting/permanent) happiness; new accurately reflects both.

TextENGNew: But you are no ordinary arrows.
While possessing the straightness and rigidity of an arrow, you also never cease to explore methods suited to the times, maintaining a vision that looks beyond the present.
Once loosed, an arrow can only fly toward its predetermined target.
But each of you is no ordinary arrow.

Even after being loosed from the bow, without neglecting your studies,@kadopting new methods,@kand implementing more effective policies, you possess the flexibility to alter your trajectory mid-flight -- you are, in essence, magical arrows.
The modern age marches ever forward.
Sometimes it marches faster than the time taken from planning to execution.
(The following portion was not in the prepared remarks and is believed to be an ad-lib by the Minister.)

For example, regarding the Hinamizawa Village Power Generation Master Plan, which has become quite contentious in recent years -- rather than forcing through a decision simply because the government made it, what is needed is the flexibility to continually reassess it against the ever-changing needs of the next generation, the region, and its residents.
changeReason: Old renders 電源基本計画 as 'Power Plant Project' but the Japanese refers to a power generation master plan (電源基本計画), and omits 村 (village); new translates the plan name more accurately.

TextENGNew: The citizens' movement surrounding the Hinamizawa Dam@kis also an expression of the people's will.@kIf we take the position that there is no need to listen because the plan has already been decided, we risk casting a dark shadow over Japan's postwar democracy.
changeReason: Old mistranslates 悪い影を落とす (cast a dark shadow) as 'shed a poor light' (nearly opposite).

TextENGNew: (The following is as per the script.)
For the lasting happiness of the citizens of Japan and the residents of XX Prefecture, please consider these policies thoroughly.
I believe, however, we have all gained something from the flexibility and foresight of the XX Party.
I've taken up much of your time,@k however, allow me to say the following to celebrate the 25th anniversary of our founding.
Chairman XX,@k members of the XX Party in attendance,@k thank you very much for today.
-- From the guest remarks of the Minister of Construction at the Prefectural Assembly Caucus and 25th Anniversary Celebration.
changeReason: Old omits the Minister of Construction and mistranslates multiple terms.
